Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                     struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                     int event, int arg1, void *arg2)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  (void)arg1;\n  (void)arg2;\n  switch(event) {\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_SETUP:\n    break;\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_PAUSE:\n    result = h3_data_pause(cf, data, (arg1 != 0));\n    break;\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_DONE: {\n    h3_data_done(cf, data);\n    break;\n  }\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_DONE_SEND: {\n    struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(data);\n    if(stream && !stream->send_closed) {\n      stream->send_closed = TRUE;\n      stream->upload_left = Curl_bufq_len(&stream->sendbuf);\n      (void)nghttp3_conn_resume_stream(ctx->h3conn, stream->id);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  case CF_CTRL_DATA_IDLE: {\n    struct h3_stream_ctx *stream = H3_STREAM_CTX(data);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"data idle\");\n    if(stream && !stream->closed) {\n      result = check_and_set_expiry(cf, data, NULL);\n      if(result)\n        CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"data idle, check_and_set_expiry -> %d\", result);\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_ctx_clear(struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx)\n{\n  struct cf_call_data save = ctx->call_data;\n\n  if(ctx->qlogfd != -1) {\n    close(ctx->qlogfd);\n  }\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL\n  if(ctx->ssl)\n    SSL_free(ctx->ssl);\n  if(ctx->sslctx)\n    SSL_CTX_free(ctx->sslctx);\n#elif defined(USE_GNUTLS)\n  if(ctx->gtls) {\n    if(ctx->gtls->cred)\n      gnutls_certificate_free_credentials(ctx->gtls->cred);\n    if(ctx->gtls->session)\n      gnutls_deinit(ctx->gtls->session);\n    free(ctx->gtls);\n  }\n#elif defined(USE_WOLFSSL)\n  if(ctx->ssl)\n    wolfSSL_free(ctx->ssl);\n  if(ctx->sslctx)\n    wolfSSL_CTX_free(ctx->sslctx);\n#endif\n  vquic_ctx_free(&ctx->q);\n  if(ctx->h3conn)\n    nghttp3_conn_del(ctx->h3conn);\n  if(ctx->qconn)\n    ngtcp2_conn_del(ctx->qconn);\n  Curl_bufcp_free(&ctx->stream_bufcp);\n\n  memset(ctx, 0, sizeof(*ctx));\n  ctx->qlogfd = -1;\n  ctx->call_data = save;\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_close(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  if(ctx && ctx->qconn) {\n    char buffer[NGTCP2_MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE];\n    ngtcp2_tstamp ts;\n    ngtcp2_ssize rc;\n\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"close\");\n    ts = timestamp();\n    rc = ngtcp2_conn_write_connection_close(ctx->qconn, NULL, /* path */\n                                            NULL, /* pkt_info */\n                                            (uint8_t *)buffer, sizeof(buffer),\n                                            &ctx->last_error, ts);\n    if(rc > 0) {\n      while((send(ctx->q.sockfd, buffer, (SEND_TYPE_ARG3)rc, 0) == -1) &&\n            SOCKERRNO == EINTR);\n    }\n\n    cf_ngtcp2_ctx_clear(ctx);\n  }\n\n  cf->connected = FALSE;\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n}\n\nstatic void cf_ngtcp2_destroy(struct Curl_cfilter *cf, struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"destroy\");\n  if(ctx) {\n    cf_ngtcp2_ctx_clear(ctx);\n    free(ctx);\n  }\n  cf->ctx = NULL;\n  /* No CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save) possible */\n  (void)save;\n}\n\n/*\n * Might be called twice for happy eyeballs.\n */\nstatic CURLcode cf_connect_start(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                 struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                 struct pkt_io_ctx *pktx)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  int rc;\n  int rv;\n  CURLcode result;\n  const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *sockaddr = NULL;\n  int qfd;\n\n  ctx->version = NGTCP2_PROTO_VER_MAX;\n  ctx->max_stream_window = H3_STREAM_WINDOW_SIZE;\n  Curl_bufcp_init(&ctx->stream_bufcp, H3_STREAM_CHUNK_SIZE,\n                  H3_STREAM_POOL_SPARES);\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL\n  result = quic_ssl_ctx(&ctx->sslctx, cf, data);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  result = quic_set_client_cert(cf, data);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n#elif defined(USE_WOLFSSL)\n  result = quic_ssl_ctx(&ctx->sslctx, cf, data);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n#endif\n\n  result = quic_init_ssl(cf, data);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  ctx->dcid.datalen = NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN;\n  result = Curl_rand(data, ctx->dcid.data, NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  ctx->scid.datalen = NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN;\n  result = Curl_rand(data, ctx->scid.data, NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  (void)Curl_qlogdir(data, ctx->scid.data, NGTCP2_MAX_CIDLEN, &qfd);\n  ctx->qlogfd = qfd; /* -1 if failure above */\n  quic_settings(ctx, data, pktx);\n\n  result = vquic_ctx_init(&ctx->q);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  Curl_cf_socket_peek(cf->next, data, &ctx->q.sockfd,\n                      &sockaddr, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL);\n  if(!sockaddr)\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n  ctx->q.local_addrlen = sizeof(ctx->q.local_addr);\n  rv = getsockname(ctx->q.sockfd, (struct sockaddr *)&ctx->q.local_addr,\n                   &ctx->q.local_addrlen);\n  if(rv == -1)\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n\n  ngtcp2_addr_init(&ctx->connected_path.local,\n                   (struct sockaddr *)&ctx->q.local_addr,\n                   ctx->q.local_addrlen);\n  ngtcp2_addr_init(&ctx->connected_path.remote,\n                   &sockaddr->sa_addr, sockaddr->addrlen);\n\n  rc = ngtcp2_conn_client_new(&ctx->qconn, &ctx->dcid, &ctx->scid,\n                              &ctx->connected_path,\n                              NGTCP2_PROTO_VER_V1, &ng_callbacks,\n                              &ctx->settings, &ctx->transport_params,\n                              NULL, cf);\n  if(rc)\n    return CURLE_QUIC_CONNECT_ERROR;\n\n#ifdef USE_GNUTLS\n  ngtcp2_conn_set_tls_native_handle(ctx->qconn, ctx->gtls->session);\n#else\n  ngtcp2_conn_set_tls_native_handle(ctx->qconn, ctx->ssl);\n#endif\n\n  ngtcp2_ccerr_default(&ctx->last_error);\n\n  ctx->conn_ref.get_conn = get_conn;\n  ctx->conn_ref.user_data = cf;\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_connect(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                  struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                  bool blocking, bool *done)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n  struct curltime now;\n  struct pkt_io_ctx pktx;\n\n  if(cf->connected) {\n    *done = TRUE;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  /* Connect the UDP filter first */\n  if(!cf->next->connected) {\n    result = Curl_conn_cf_connect(cf->next, data, blocking, done);\n    if(result || !*done)\n      return result;\n  }\n\n  *done = FALSE;\n  now = Curl_now();\n  pktx_init(&pktx, cf, data);\n\n  CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n\n  if(ctx->reconnect_at.tv_sec && Curl_timediff(now, ctx->reconnect_at) < 0) {\n    /* Not time yet to attempt the next connect */\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"waiting for reconnect time\");\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  if(!ctx->qconn) {\n    ctx->started_at = now;\n    result = cf_connect_start(cf, data, &pktx);\n    if(result)\n      goto out;\n    result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx);\n    /* we do not expect to be able to recv anything yet */\n    goto out;\n  }\n\n  result = cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, &pktx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  result = cf_progress_egress(cf, data, &pktx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  if(ngtcp2_conn_get_handshake_completed(ctx->qconn)) {\n    ctx->handshake_at = now;\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"handshake complete after %dms\",\n               (int)Curl_timediff(now, ctx->started_at));\n    result = qng_verify_peer(cf, data);\n    if(!result) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"peer verified\");\n      cf->connected = TRUE;\n      cf->conn->alpn = CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3;\n      *done = TRUE;\n      connkeep(cf->conn, \"HTTP/3 default\");\n    }\n  }\n\nout:\n  if(result == CURLE_RECV_ERROR && ctx->qconn &&\n     ngtcp2_conn_in_draining_period(ctx->qconn)) {\n    /* When a QUIC server instance is shutting down, it may send us a\n     * CONNECTION_CLOSE right away. Our connection then enters the DRAINING\n     * state.\n     * This may be a stopping of the service or it may be that the server\n     * is reloading and a new instance will start serving soon.\n     * In any case, we tear down our socket and start over with a new one.\n     * We re-open the underlying UDP cf right now, but do not start\n     * connecting until called again.\n     */\n    int reconn_delay_ms = 200;\n\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"connect, remote closed, reconnect after %dms\",\n                reconn_delay_ms);\n    Curl_conn_cf_close(cf->next, data);\n    cf_ngtcp2_ctx_clear(ctx);\n    result = Curl_conn_cf_connect(cf->next, data, FALSE, done);\n    if(!result && *done) {\n      *done = FALSE;\n      ctx->reconnect_at = now;\n      ctx->reconnect_at.tv_usec += reconn_delay_ms * 1000;\n      Curl_expire(data, reconn_delay_ms, EXPIRE_QUIC);\n      result = CURLE_OK;\n    }\n  }\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS\n  if(result) {\n    const char *r_ip = NULL;\n    int r_port = 0;\n\n    Curl_cf_socket_peek(cf->next, data, NULL, NULL,\n                        &r_ip, &r_port, NULL, NULL);\n    infof(data, \"QUIC connect to %s port %u failed: %s\",\n          r_ip, r_port, curl_easy_strerror(result));\n  }\n#endif\n  if(!result && ctx->qconn) {\n    result = check_and_set_expiry(cf, data, &pktx);\n  }\n  if(result || *done)\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"connect -> %d, done=%d\", result, *done);\n  CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode cf_ngtcp2_query(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                int query, int *pres1, void *pres2)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = cf->ctx;\n  struct cf_call_data save;\n\n  switch(query) {\n  case CF_QUERY_MAX_CONCURRENT: {\n    const ngtcp2_transport_params *rp;\n    DEBUGASSERT(pres1);\n\n    CF_DATA_SAVE(save, cf, data);\n    rp = ngtcp2_conn_get_remote_transport_params(ctx->qconn);\n    if(rp)\n      *pres1 = (rp->initial_max_streams_bidi > INT_MAX)?\n                 INT_MAX : (int)rp->initial_max_streams_bidi;\n    else  /* not arrived yet? */\n      *pres1 = Curl_multi_max_concurrent_streams(data->multi);\n    CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"query max_conncurrent -> %d\", *pres1);\n    CF_DATA_RESTORE(cf, save);\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  case CF_QUERY_CONNECT_REPLY_MS:\n    if(ctx->got_first_byte) {\n      timediff_t ms = Curl_timediff(ctx->first_byte_at, ctx->started_at);\n      *pres1 = (ms < INT_MAX)? (int)ms : INT_MAX;\n    }\n    else\n      *pres1 = -1;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  case CF_QUERY_TIMER_CONNECT: {\n    struct curltime *when = pres2;\n    if(ctx->got_first_byte)\n      *when = ctx->first_byte_at;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  case CF_QUERY_TIMER_APPCONNECT: {\n    struct curltime *when = pres2;\n    if(cf->connected)\n      *when = ctx->handshake_at;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n  return cf->next?\n    cf->next->cft->query(cf->next, data, query, pres1, pres2) :\n    CURLE_UNKNOWN_OPTION;\n}\n\nstatic bool cf_ngtcp2_conn_is_alive(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                    struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                    bool *input_pending)\n{\n  bool alive = TRUE;\n\n  *input_pending = FALSE;\n  if(!cf->next || !cf->next->cft->is_alive(cf->next, data, input_pending))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(*input_pending) {\n    /* This happens before we've sent off a request and the connection is\n       not in use by any other transfer, there shouldn't be any data here,\n       only \"protocol frames\" */\n    *input_pending = FALSE;\n    if(cf_progress_ingress(cf, data, NULL))\n      alive = FALSE;\n    else {\n      alive = TRUE;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return alive;\n}\n\nstruct Curl_cftype Curl_cft_http3 = {\n  \"HTTP/3\",\n  CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT | CF_TYPE_SSL | CF_TYPE_MULTIPLEX,\n  0,\n  cf_ngtcp2_destroy,\n  cf_ngtcp2_connect,\n  cf_ngtcp2_close,\n  Curl_cf_def_get_host,\n  cf_ngtcp2_get_select_socks,\n  cf_ngtcp2_data_pending,\n  cf_ngtcp2_send,\n  cf_ngtcp2_recv,\n  cf_ngtcp2_data_event,\n  cf_ngtcp2_conn_is_alive,\n  Curl_cf_def_conn_keep_alive,\n  cf_ngtcp2_query,\n};\n\nCURLcode Curl_cf_ngtcp2_create(struct Curl_cfilter **pcf,\n                               struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn,\n                               const struct Curl_addrinfo *ai)\n{\n  struct cf_ngtcp2_ctx *ctx = NULL;\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = NULL, *udp_cf = NULL;\n  CURLcode result;\n\n  (void)data;\n  ctx = calloc(sizeof(*ctx), 1);\n  if(!ctx) {\n    result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n    goto out;\n  }\n  ctx->qlogfd = -1;\n  cf_ngtcp2_ctx_clear(ctx);\n\n  result = Curl_cf_create(&cf, &Curl_cft_http3, ctx);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  result = Curl_cf_udp_create(&udp_cf, data, conn, ai, TRNSPRT_QUIC);\n  if(result)\n    goto out;\n\n  cf->conn = conn;\n  udp_cf->conn = cf->conn;\n  udp_cf->sockindex = cf->sockindex;\n  cf->next = udp_cf;\n\nout:\n  *pcf = (!result)? cf : NULL;\n  if(result) {\n    if(udp_cf)\n      Curl_conn_cf_discard_sub(cf, udp_cf, data, TRUE);\n    Curl_safefree(cf);\n    Curl_safefree(ctx);\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nbool Curl_conn_is_ngtcp2(const struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         const struct connectdata *conn,\n                         int sockindex)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = conn? conn->cfilter[sockindex] : NULL;\n\n  (void)data;\n  for(; cf; cf = cf->next) {\n    if(cf->cft == &Curl_cft_http3)\n      return TRUE;\n    if(cf->cft->flags & CF_TYPE_IP_CONNECT)\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n#endif\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     goto out;",
        "   }",
        " ",
        "+  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities: send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame if set */",
        "+  if(data->set.priority.urgency || data->set.priority.incremental) {",
        "+    nghttp3_priority priority;",
        "+    priority.urgency = data->set.priority.urgency;",
        "+    priority.incremental = data->set.priority.incremental ? 1 : 0;",
        "+    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,",
        "+                                                  &priority);",
        "+    if(rc) {",
        "+      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\"PRId64\"] priority update failed -> %d (%s)\",",
        "+                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));",
        "+      /* don't treat as fatal, continue without priority */",
        "+    }",
        "+  }",
        "+",
        "   if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {",
        "     infof(data, \"[HTTP/3] [%\" PRId64 \"] OPENED stream for %s\",",
        "           stream->id, data->state.url);"
      ],

      "newStart": 1760,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 1760
    }

  "userModified": false

68. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/unit/unit1398.c",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n#define CURL_NO_FMT_CHECKS\n\n#include \"curlcheck.h\"\n\n#include \"curl/mprintf.h\"\n\nstatic CURLcode unit_setup(void) {return CURLE_OK;}\nstatic void unit_stop(void) {}\n\nUNITTEST_START\n\nint rc;\nchar buf[3] = {'b', 'u', 'g'};\nconst char *str = \"bug\";\nint width = 3;\nchar output[24];\n\n/*#define curl_msnprintf snprintf */\n\n/* without a trailing zero */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 4, \"%.*s\", width, buf);\nfail_unless(rc == 3, \"return code should be 3\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"bug\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* with a trailing zero */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 4, \"%.*s\", width, str);\nfail_unless(rc == 3, \"return code should be 3\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"bug\"), \"wrong output\");\n\nwidth = 2;\n/* one byte less */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 4, \"%.*s\", width, buf);\nfail_unless(rc == 2, \"return code should be 2\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"bu\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* string with larger precision */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 8, \"%.8s\", str);\nfail_unless(rc == 3, \"return code should be 3\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"bug\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* longer string with precision */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 8, \"%.3s\", \"0123456789\");\nfail_unless(rc == 3, \"return code should be 3\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"012\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* negative width */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 8, \"%-8s\", str);\nfail_unless(rc == 7, \"return code should be 7\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"bug    \"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* larger width that string length */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 8, \"%8s\", str);\nfail_unless(rc == 7, \"return code should be 7\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"     bu\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* output a number in a limited output */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 4, \"%d\", 10240);\nfail_unless(rc == 3, \"return code should be 3\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"102\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* padded strings */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 16, \"%8s%8s\", str, str);\nfail_unless(rc == 15, \"return code should be 15\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"     bug     bu\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* padded numbers */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 16, \"%8d%8d\", 1234, 5678);\nfail_unless(rc == 15, \"return code should be 15\");\nfail_unless(!strcmp(output, \"    1234    567\"), \"wrong output\");\n\n/* double precision */\nrc = curl_msnprintf(output, 24, \"%.*1$.99d\", 3, 5678);\nfail_unless(rc == 0, \"return code should be 0\");\n\nUNITTEST_STOP\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/tests/unit/unit1398.c",
    "numLines": 99,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 99
  },
  "type": "text"
}

70. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n#include \"curlcheck.h\"\n\nstatic CURLcode unit_setup(void) {\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic void unit_stop(void) {\n}\n\nUNITTEST_START\n\n{\n  CURL *curl = curl_easy_init();\n  fail_unless(curl != NULL, \"curl_easy_init failed\");\n\n  /* Test 1: Valid H3 priority with urgency only */\n  CURLcode res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=3\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=3\");\n\n  /* Test 2: Valid H3 priority with urgency and incremental flag */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=5,i\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=5,i\");\n\n  /* Test 3: Minimum urgency value */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=0\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=0\");\n\n  /* Test 4: Maximum urgency value */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=7\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=7\");\n\n  /* Test 5: Invalid urgency (too high) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=8\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT,\n              \"should reject urgency value > 7\");\n\n  /* Test 6: Invalid format */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"invalid\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT,\n              \"should reject invalid format\");\n\n  /* Test 7: Null priority string (clear setting) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, NULL);\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to clear H3 priority\");\n\n  curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n}\n\nUNITTEST_STOP\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/unit/unit1400.c"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n#include \"curlcheck.h\"\n\nstatic CURLcode unit_setup(void) {\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic void unit_stop(void) {\n}\n\nUNITTEST_START\n\n{\n  CURL *curl = curl_easy_init();\n  fail_unless(curl != NULL, \"curl_easy_init failed\");\n\n  /* Test 1: Valid H3 priority with urgency only */\n  CURLcode res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=3\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=3\");\n\n  /* Test 2: Valid H3 priority with urgency and incremental flag */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=5,i\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=5,i\");\n\n  /* Test 3: Minimum urgency value */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=0\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=0\");\n\n  /* Test 4: Maximum urgency value */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=7\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to set H3 priority u=7\");\n\n  /* Test 5: Invalid urgency (too high) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=8\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT,\n              \"should reject urgency value > 7\");\n\n  /* Test 6: Invalid format */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"invalid\");\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT,\n              \"should reject invalid format\");\n\n  /* Test 7: Null priority string (clear setting) */\n  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, NULL);\n  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, \"failed to clear H3 priority\");\n\n  curl_easy_cleanup(curl);\n}\n\nUNITTEST_STOP\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/tests/unit/unit1400.c",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n#include \"curl_setup.h\"\n\n#include <limits.h>\n\n#ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n#include <netinet/in.h>\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_LINUX_TCP_H\n#include <linux/tcp.h>\n#elif defined(HAVE_NETINET_TCP_H)\n#include <netinet/tcp.h>\n#endif\n\n#include \"urldata.h\"\n#include \"url.h\"\n#include \"progress.h\"\n#include \"content_encoding.h\"\n#include \"strcase.h\"\n#include \"share.h\"\n#include \"vtls/vtls.h\"\n#include \"warnless.h\"\n#include \"sendf.h\"\n#include \"http2.h\"\n#include \"setopt.h\"\n#include \"multiif.h\"\n#include \"altsvc.h\"\n#include \"hsts.h\"\n\n/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */\n#include \"curl_printf.h\"\n#include \"curl_memory.h\"\n#include \"memdebug.h\"\n\nCURLcode Curl_setstropt(char **charp, const char *s)\n{",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/setopt.c",

    "startLine": 1,

77. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c",
  "limit": 20,
  "offset": 1760
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    goto out;\n  }\n\n  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities: send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame if set */\n  if(data->set.priority.urgency || data->set.priority.incremental) {\n    nghttp3_priority priority;\n    priority.urgency = data->set.priority.urgency;\n    priority.incremental = data->set.priority.incremental ? 1 : 0;\n    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,\n                                                  &priority);\n    if(rc) {\n      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"h3sid[%\"PRId64\"] priority update failed -> %d (%s)\",\n                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));\n      /* don't treat as fatal, continue without priority */\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(Curl_trc_is_verbose(data)) {\n    infof(data, \"[HTTP/3] [%\" PRId64 \"] OPENED stream for %s\",\n          stream->id, data->state.url);",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c",
    "numLines": 20,
    "startLine": 1760,
    "totalLines": 2772
  },
  "type": "text"
}
78. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/urldata.h",
  "new_string": "#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */\n  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */\n  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */\n  BIT(priority_set);      /* flag indicating priority was explicitly set */\n#endif",
  "old_string": "#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */\n  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */\n  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */\n#endif",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/lib/urldata.h",
  "newString": "#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */\n  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */\n  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */\n  BIT(priority_set);      /* flag indicating priority was explicitly set */\n#endif",
  "oldString": "#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */\n  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */\n  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "#ifndef HEADER_CURL_URLDATA_H\n#define HEADER_CURL_URLDATA_H\n/***************************************************************************\n *                                  _   _ ____  _\n *  Project                     ___| | | |  _ \\| |\n *                             / __| | | | |_) | |\n *                            | (__| |_| |  _ <| |___\n *                             \\___|\\___/|_| \\_\\_____|\n *\n * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.\n *\n * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which\n * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms\n * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html.\n *\n * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell\n * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is\n * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file.\n *\n * This software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY\n * KIND, either express or implied.\n *\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl\n *\n ***************************************************************************/\n\n/* This file is for lib internal stuff */\n\n#include \"curl_setup.h\"\n\n#define PORT_FTP 21\n#define PORT_FTPS 990\n#define PORT_TELNET 23\n#define PORT_HTTP 80\n#define PORT_HTTPS 443\n#define PORT_DICT 2628\n#define PORT_LDAP 389\n#define PORT_LDAPS 636\n#define PORT_TFTP 69\n#define PORT_SSH 22\n#define PORT_IMAP 143\n#define PORT_IMAPS 993\n#define PORT_POP3 110\n#define PORT_POP3S 995\n#define PORT_SMB 445\n#define PORT_SMBS 445\n#define PORT_SMTP 25\n#define PORT_SMTPS 465 /* sometimes called SSMTP */\n#define PORT_RTSP 554\n#define PORT_RTMP 1935\n#define PORT_RTMPT PORT_HTTP\n#define PORT_RTMPS PORT_HTTPS\n#define PORT_GOPHER 70\n#define PORT_MQTT 1883\n\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n/* CURLPROTO_GOPHERS (29) is the highest publicly used protocol bit number,\n * the rest are internal information. If we use higher bits we only do this on\n * platforms that have a >= 64 bit type and then we use such a type for the\n * protocol fields in the protocol handler.\n */\n#define CURLPROTO_WS     (1<<30)\n#define CURLPROTO_WSS    ((curl_prot_t)1<<31)\n#else\n#define CURLPROTO_WS 0\n#define CURLPROTO_WSS 0\n#endif\n\n/* This should be undefined once we need bit 32 or higher */\n#define PROTO_TYPE_SMALL\n\n#ifndef PROTO_TYPE_SMALL\ntypedef curl_off_t curl_prot_t;\n#else\ntypedef unsigned int curl_prot_t;\n#endif\n\n/* This mask is for all the old protocols that are provided and defined in the\n   public header and shall exclude protocols added since which are not exposed\n   in the API */\n#define CURLPROTO_MASK   (0x3ffffff)\n\n#define DICT_MATCH \"/MATCH:\"\n#define DICT_MATCH2 \"/M:\"\n#define DICT_MATCH3 \"/FIND:\"\n#define DICT_DEFINE \"/DEFINE:\"\n#define DICT_DEFINE2 \"/D:\"\n#define DICT_DEFINE3 \"/LOOKUP:\"\n\n#define CURL_DEFAULT_USER \"anonymous\"\n#define CURL_DEFAULT_PASSWORD \"ftp@example.com\"\n\n/* Convenience defines for checking protocols or their SSL based version. Each\n   protocol handler should only ever have a single CURLPROTO_ in its protocol\n   field. */\n#define PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP (CURLPROTO_HTTP|CURLPROTO_HTTPS|CURLPROTO_WS| \\\n                           CURLPROTO_WSS)\n#define PROTO_FAMILY_FTP  (CURLPROTO_FTP|CURLPROTO_FTPS)\n#define PROTO_FAMILY_POP3 (CURLPROTO_POP3|CURLPROTO_POP3S)\n#define PROTO_FAMILY_SMB  (CURLPROTO_SMB|CURLPROTO_SMBS)\n#define PROTO_FAMILY_SMTP (CURLPROTO_SMTP|CURLPROTO_SMTPS)\n#define PROTO_FAMILY_SSH  (CURLPROTO_SCP|CURLPROTO_SFTP)\n\n#if !defined(CURL_DISABLE_FTP) || defined(USE_SSH) ||   \\\n  !defined(CURL_DISABLE_POP3)\n/* these protocols support CURLOPT_DIRLISTONLY */\n#define CURL_LIST_ONLY_PROTOCOL 1\n#endif\n\n#define DEFAULT_CONNCACHE_SIZE 5\n\n/* length of longest IPv6 address string including the trailing null */\n#define MAX_IPADR_LEN sizeof(\"ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:255.255.255.255\")\n\n/* Default FTP/IMAP etc response timeout in milliseconds */\n#define RESP_TIMEOUT (120*1000)\n\n/* Max string input length is a precaution against abuse and to detect junk\n   input easier and better. */\n#define CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH 8000000\n\n\n#include \"cookie.h\"\n#include \"psl.h\"\n#include \"formdata.h\"\n\n#ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN_H\n#include <netinet/in.h>\n#endif\n#ifdef HAVE_NETINET_IN6_H\n#include <netinet/in6.h>\n#endif\n\n#include \"timeval.h\"\n\n#include <curl/curl.h>\n\n#include \"http_chunks.h\" /* for the structs and enum stuff */\n#include \"hostip.h\"\n#include \"hash.h\"\n#include \"splay.h\"\n#include \"dynbuf.h\"\n#include \"dynhds.h\"\n\n/* return the count of bytes sent, or -1 on error */\ntypedef ssize_t (Curl_send)(struct Curl_easy *data,   /* transfer */\n                            int sockindex,            /* socketindex */\n                            const void *buf,          /* data to write */\n                            size_t len,               /* max amount to write */\n                            CURLcode *err);           /* error to return */\n\n/* return the count of bytes read, or -1 on error */\ntypedef ssize_t (Curl_recv)(struct Curl_easy *data,   /* transfer */\n                            int sockindex,            /* socketindex */\n                            char *buf,                /* store data here */\n                            size_t len,               /* max amount to read */\n                            CURLcode *err);           /* error to return */\n\n#ifdef USE_HYPER\ntypedef CURLcode (*Curl_datastream)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                    struct connectdata *conn,\n                                    int *didwhat,\n                                    bool *done,\n                                    int select_res);\n#endif\n\n#include \"mime.h\"\n#include \"imap.h\"\n#include \"pop3.h\"\n#include \"smtp.h\"\n#include \"ftp.h\"\n#include \"file.h\"\n#include \"vssh/ssh.h\"\n#include \"http.h\"\n#include \"rtsp.h\"\n#include \"smb.h\"\n#include \"mqtt.h\"\n#include \"ftplistparser.h\"\n#include \"multihandle.h\"\n#include \"c-hyper.h\"\n#include \"cf-socket.h\"\n\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n# ifdef HAVE_GSSGNU\n#  include <gss.h>\n# elif defined HAVE_GSSAPI_GSSAPI_H\n#  include <gssapi/gssapi.h>\n# else\n#  include <gssapi.h>\n# endif\n# ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI_GSSAPI_GENERIC_H\n#  include <gssapi/gssapi_generic.h>\n# endif\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_LIBSSH2\n#include <libssh2.h>\n#include <libssh2_sftp.h>\n#endif /* USE_LIBSSH2 */\n\n#define READBUFFER_SIZE CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\n#define READBUFFER_MAX  CURL_MAX_READ_SIZE\n#define READBUFFER_MIN  1024\n\n/* The default upload buffer size, should not be smaller than\n   CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE, as it needs to hold a full buffer as could be sent in\n   a write callback.\n\n   The size was 16KB for many years but was bumped to 64KB because it makes\n   libcurl able to do significantly faster uploads in some circumstances. Even\n   larger buffers can help further, but this is deemed a fair memory/speed\n   compromise. */\n#define UPLOADBUFFER_DEFAULT 65536\n#define UPLOADBUFFER_MAX (2*1024*1024)\n#define UPLOADBUFFER_MIN CURL_MAX_WRITE_SIZE\n\n#define CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER 0xc0dedbadU\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n/* On a debug build, we want to fail hard on easy handles that\n * are not NULL, but no longer have the MAGIC touch. This gives\n * us early warning on things only discovered by valgrind otherwise. */\n#define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\n  (((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))? TRUE: \\\n  (DEBUGASSERT(!(x)), FALSE))\n#else\n#define GOOD_EASY_HANDLE(x) \\\n  ((x) && ((x)->magic == CURLEASY_MAGIC_NUMBER))\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n/* Types needed for krb5-ftp connections */\nstruct krb5buffer {\n  void *data;\n  size_t size;\n  size_t index;\n  BIT(eof_flag);\n};\n\nenum protection_level {\n  PROT_NONE, /* first in list */\n  PROT_CLEAR,\n  PROT_SAFE,\n  PROT_CONFIDENTIAL,\n  PROT_PRIVATE,\n  PROT_CMD,\n  PROT_LAST /* last in list */\n};\n#endif\n\n/* enum for the nonblocking SSL connection state machine */\ntypedef enum {\n  ssl_connect_1,\n  ssl_connect_2,\n  ssl_connect_2_reading,\n  ssl_connect_2_writing,\n  ssl_connect_3,\n  ssl_connect_done\n} ssl_connect_state;\n\ntypedef enum {\n  ssl_connection_none,\n  ssl_connection_negotiating,\n  ssl_connection_complete\n} ssl_connection_state;\n\n/* SSL backend-specific data; declared differently by each SSL backend */\nstruct ssl_backend_data;\n\nstruct ssl_primary_config {\n  char *CApath;          /* certificate dir (doesn't work on windows) */\n  char *CAfile;          /* certificate to verify peer against */\n  char *issuercert;      /* optional issuer certificate filename */\n  char *clientcert;\n  char *cipher_list;     /* list of ciphers to use */\n  char *cipher_list13;   /* list of TLS 1.3 cipher suites to use */\n  char *pinned_key;\n  char *CRLfile;         /* CRL to check certificate revocation */\n  struct curl_blob *cert_blob;\n  struct curl_blob *ca_info_blob;\n  struct curl_blob *issuercert_blob;\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n  char *username; /* TLS username (for, e.g., SRP) */\n  char *password; /* TLS password (for, e.g., SRP) */\n#endif\n  char *curves;          /* list of curves to use */\n  unsigned char ssl_options;  /* the CURLOPT_SSL_OPTIONS bitmask */\n  unsigned int version_max; /* max supported version the client wants to use */\n  unsigned char version;    /* what version the client wants to use */\n  BIT(verifypeer);       /* set TRUE if this is desired */\n  BIT(verifyhost);       /* set TRUE if CN/SAN must match hostname */\n  BIT(verifystatus);     /* set TRUE if certificate status must be checked */\n  BIT(sessionid);        /* cache session IDs or not */\n};\n\nstruct ssl_config_data {\n  struct ssl_primary_config primary;\n  long certverifyresult; /* result from the certificate verification */\n  curl_ssl_ctx_callback fsslctx; /* function to initialize ssl ctx */\n  void *fsslctxp;        /* parameter for call back */\n  char *cert_type; /* format for certificate (default: PEM)*/\n  char *key; /* private key file name */\n  struct curl_blob *key_blob;\n  char *key_type; /* format for private key (default: PEM) */\n  char *key_passwd; /* plain text private key password */\n  BIT(certinfo);     /* gather lots of certificate info */\n  BIT(falsestart);\n  BIT(enable_beast); /* allow this flaw for interoperability's sake */\n  BIT(no_revoke);    /* disable SSL certificate revocation checks */\n  BIT(no_partialchain); /* don't accept partial certificate chains */\n  BIT(revoke_best_effort); /* ignore SSL revocation offline/missing revocation\n                              list errors */\n  BIT(native_ca_store); /* use the native ca store of operating system */\n  BIT(auto_client_cert);   /* automatically locate and use a client\n                              certificate for authentication (Schannel) */\n};\n\nstruct ssl_general_config {\n  size_t max_ssl_sessions; /* SSL session id cache size */\n  int ca_cache_timeout;  /* Certificate store cache timeout (seconds) */\n};\n\n/* information stored about one single SSL session */\nstruct Curl_ssl_session {\n  char *name;       /* host name for which this ID was used */\n  char *conn_to_host; /* host name for the connection (may be NULL) */\n  const char *scheme; /* protocol scheme used */\n  void *sessionid;  /* as returned from the SSL layer */\n  size_t idsize;    /* if known, otherwise 0 */\n  long age;         /* just a number, the higher the more recent */\n  int remote_port;  /* remote port */\n  int conn_to_port; /* remote port for the connection (may be -1) */\n  struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config; /* setup for this session */\n};\n\n#ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n#include \"curl_sspi.h\"\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH\n/* Struct used for Digest challenge-response authentication */\nstruct digestdata {\n#if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  BYTE *input_token;\n  size_t input_token_len;\n  CtxtHandle *http_context;\n  /* copy of user/passwd used to make the identity for http_context.\n     either may be NULL. */\n  char *user;\n  char *passwd;\n#else\n  char *nonce;\n  char *cnonce;\n  char *realm;\n  char *opaque;\n  char *qop;\n  char *algorithm;\n  int nc; /* nonce count */\n  unsigned char algo;\n  BIT(stale); /* set true for re-negotiation */\n  BIT(userhash);\n#endif\n};\n#endif\n\ntypedef enum {\n  NTLMSTATE_NONE,\n  NTLMSTATE_TYPE1,\n  NTLMSTATE_TYPE2,\n  NTLMSTATE_TYPE3,\n  NTLMSTATE_LAST\n} curlntlm;\n\ntypedef enum {\n  GSS_AUTHNONE,\n  GSS_AUTHRECV,\n  GSS_AUTHSENT,\n  GSS_AUTHDONE,\n  GSS_AUTHSUCC\n} curlnegotiate;\n\n/* Struct used for GSSAPI (Kerberos V5) authentication */\n#if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)\nstruct kerberos5data {\n#if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  CredHandle *credentials;\n  CtxtHandle *context;\n  TCHAR *spn;\n  SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\n  SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\n  size_t token_max;\n  BYTE *output_token;\n#else\n  gss_ctx_id_t context;\n  gss_name_t spn;\n#endif\n};\n#endif\n\n/* Struct used for SCRAM-SHA-1 authentication */\n#ifdef USE_GSASL\n#include <gsasl.h>\nstruct gsasldata {\n  Gsasl *ctx;\n  Gsasl_session *client;\n};\n#endif\n\n/* Struct used for NTLM challenge-response authentication */\n#if defined(USE_NTLM)\nstruct ntlmdata {\n#ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n/* The sslContext is used for the Schannel bindings. The\n * api is available on the Windows 7 SDK and later.\n */\n#ifdef SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS\n  CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n#endif\n  CredHandle *credentials;\n  CtxtHandle *context;\n  SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\n  SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\n  size_t token_max;\n  BYTE *output_token;\n  BYTE *input_token;\n  size_t input_token_len;\n  TCHAR *spn;\n#else\n  unsigned int flags;\n  unsigned char nonce[8];\n  unsigned int target_info_len;\n  void *target_info; /* TargetInfo received in the ntlm type-2 message */\n\n#if defined(NTLM_WB_ENABLED)\n  /* used for communication with Samba's winbind daemon helper ntlm_auth */\n  curl_socket_t ntlm_auth_hlpr_socket;\n  pid_t ntlm_auth_hlpr_pid;\n  char *challenge; /* The received base64 encoded ntlm type-2 message */\n  char *response;  /* The generated base64 ntlm type-1/type-3 message */\n#endif\n#endif\n};\n#endif\n\n/* Struct used for Negotiate (SPNEGO) authentication */\n#ifdef USE_SPNEGO\nstruct negotiatedata {\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  OM_uint32 status;\n  gss_ctx_id_t context;\n  gss_name_t spn;\n  gss_buffer_desc output_token;\n#else\n#ifdef USE_WINDOWS_SSPI\n#ifdef SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS\n  CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n#endif\n  DWORD status;\n  CredHandle *credentials;\n  CtxtHandle *context;\n  SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY identity;\n  SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY *p_identity;\n  TCHAR *spn;\n  size_t token_max;\n  BYTE *output_token;\n  size_t output_token_length;\n#endif\n#endif\n  BIT(noauthpersist);\n  BIT(havenoauthpersist);\n  BIT(havenegdata);\n  BIT(havemultiplerequests);\n};\n#endif\n\n#ifdef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n#define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) 0\n#else\n#define CONN_IS_PROXIED(x) x->bits.proxy\n#endif\n\n/*\n * Boolean values that concerns this connection.\n */\nstruct ConnectBits {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  BIT(httpproxy);  /* if set, this transfer is done through an HTTP proxy */\n  BIT(socksproxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a socks proxy */\n  BIT(proxy_user_passwd); /* user+password for the proxy? */\n  BIT(tunnel_proxy);  /* if CONNECT is used to \"tunnel\" through the proxy.\n                         This is implicit when SSL-protocols are used through\n                         proxies, but can also be enabled explicitly by\n                         apps */\n  BIT(proxy_connect_closed); /* TRUE if a proxy disconnected the connection\n                                in a CONNECT request with auth, so that\n                                libcurl should reconnect and continue. */\n  BIT(proxy); /* if set, this transfer is done through a proxy - any type */\n#endif\n  /* always modify bits.close with the connclose() and connkeep() macros! */\n  BIT(close); /* if set, we close the connection after this request */\n  BIT(reuse); /* if set, this is a reused connection */\n  BIT(altused); /* this is an alt-svc \"redirect\" */\n  BIT(conn_to_host); /* if set, this connection has a \"connect to host\"\n                        that overrides the host in the URL */\n  BIT(conn_to_port); /* if set, this connection has a \"connect to port\"\n                        that overrides the port in the URL (remote port) */\n  BIT(ipv6_ip); /* we communicate with a remote site specified with pure IPv6\n                   IP address */\n  BIT(ipv6);    /* we communicate with a site using an IPv6 address */\n  BIT(do_more); /* this is set TRUE if the ->curl_do_more() function is\n                   supposed to be called, after ->curl_do() */\n  BIT(protoconnstart);/* the protocol layer has STARTED its operation after\n                         the TCP layer connect */\n  BIT(retry);         /* this connection is about to get closed and then\n                         re-attempted at another connection. */\n  BIT(authneg);       /* TRUE when the auth phase has started, which means\n                         that we are creating a request with an auth header,\n                         but it is not the final request in the auth\n                         negotiation. */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n  BIT(ftp_use_epsv);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPSV, but if we find out\n                         EPSV doesn't work we disable it for the forthcoming\n                         requests */\n  BIT(ftp_use_eprt);  /* As set with CURLOPT_FTP_USE_EPRT, but if we find out\n                         EPRT doesn't work we disable it for the forthcoming\n                         requests */\n  BIT(ftp_use_data_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the data connection */\n  BIT(ftp_use_control_ssl); /* Enabled SSL for the control connection */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_NETRC\n  BIT(netrc);         /* name+password provided by netrc */\n#endif\n  BIT(bound); /* set true if bind() has already been done on this socket/\n                 connection */\n  BIT(multiplex); /* connection is multiplexed */\n  BIT(tcp_fastopen); /* use TCP Fast Open */\n  BIT(tls_enable_alpn); /* TLS ALPN extension? */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  BIT(doh);\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  BIT(abstract_unix_socket);\n#endif\n  BIT(tls_upgraded);\n  BIT(sock_accepted); /* TRUE if the SECONDARYSOCKET was created with\n                         accept() */\n  BIT(parallel_connect); /* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\n                            started (happy eyeballs) */\n};\n\nstruct hostname {\n  char *rawalloc; /* allocated \"raw\" version of the name */\n  char *encalloc; /* allocated IDN-encoded version of the name */\n  char *name;     /* name to use internally, might be encoded, might be raw */\n  const char *dispname; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\n};\n\n/*\n * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\n */\n\n#define KEEP_NONE  0\n#define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\n#define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\n                                 might still be data to read */\n#define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\n                                  might still be data to write */\n#define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\n#define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\n\n#define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\n#define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\n\n#if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\n#define USE_CURL_ASYNC\nstruct Curl_async {\n  char *hostname;\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\n  struct thread_data *tdata;\n  void *resolver; /* resolver state, if it is used in the URL state -\n                     ares_channel e.g. */\n  int port;\n  int status; /* if done is TRUE, this is the status from the callback */\n  BIT(done);  /* set TRUE when the lookup is complete */\n};\n\n#endif\n\n#define FIRSTSOCKET     0\n#define SECONDARYSOCKET 1\n\nenum expect100 {\n  EXP100_SEND_DATA,           /* enough waiting, just send the body now */\n  EXP100_AWAITING_CONTINUE,   /* waiting for the 100 Continue header */\n  EXP100_SENDING_REQUEST,     /* still sending the request but will wait for\n                                 the 100 header once done with the request */\n  EXP100_FAILED               /* used on 417 Expectation Failed */\n};\n\nenum upgrade101 {\n  UPGR101_INIT,               /* default state */\n  UPGR101_WS,                 /* upgrade to WebSockets requested */\n  UPGR101_H2,                 /* upgrade to HTTP/2 requested */\n  UPGR101_RECEIVED,           /* 101 response received */\n  UPGR101_WORKING             /* talking upgraded protocol */\n};\n\nenum doh_slots {\n  /* Explicit values for first two symbols so as to match hard-coded\n   * constants in existing code\n   */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V4 = 0, /* make 'V4' stand out for readability */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOT_IPADDR_V6 = 1, /* 'V6' likewise */\n\n  /* Space here for (possibly build-specific) additional slot definitions */\n\n  /* for example */\n  /* #ifdef WANT_DOH_FOOBAR_TXT */\n  /*   DOH_PROBE_SLOT_FOOBAR_TXT, */\n  /* #endif */\n\n  /* AFTER all slot definitions, establish how many we have */\n  DOH_PROBE_SLOTS\n};\n\n/*\n * Request specific data in the easy handle (Curl_easy).  Previously,\n * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\n * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\n * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\n * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\n */\nstruct SingleRequest {\n  curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\n  curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\n                             -1 means unlimited */\n  curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\n  curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\n\n  curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\n                                    header and not body */\n  struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\n  unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\n                                    headers) */\n  unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\n  unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\n                                     we check if anything has been transferred\n                                     at the end of a connection. We use this\n                                     counter to make only a 100 reply (without\n                                     a following second response code) result\n                                     in a CURLE_GOT_NOTHING error code */\n  enum {\n    HEADER_NORMAL,              /* no bad header at all */\n    HEADER_PARTHEADER,          /* part of the chunk is a bad header, the rest\n                                   is normal data */\n    HEADER_ALLBAD               /* all was believed to be header */\n  } badheader;                  /* the header was deemed bad and will be\n                                   written as body */\n  int headerline;               /* counts header lines to better track the\n                                   first one */\n  char *str;                    /* within buf */\n  curl_off_t offset;            /* possible resume offset read from the\n                                   Content-Range: header */\n  int httpcode;                 /* error code from the 'HTTP/1.? XXX' or\n                                   'RTSP/1.? XXX' line */\n  int keepon;\n  struct curltime start100;      /* time stamp to wait for the 100 code from */\n  enum expect100 exp100;        /* expect 100 continue state */\n  enum upgrade101 upgr101;      /* 101 upgrade state */\n\n  /* Content unencoding stack. See sec 3.5, RFC2616. */\n  struct contenc_writer *writer_stack;\n  time_t timeofdoc;\n  long bodywrites;\n  char *location;   /* This points to an allocated version of the Location:\n                       header data */\n  char *newurl;     /* Set to the new URL to use when a redirect or a retry is\n                       wanted */\n\n  /* 'upload_present' is used to keep a byte counter of how much data there is\n     still left in the buffer, aimed for upload. */\n  ssize_t upload_present;\n\n  /* 'upload_fromhere' is used as a read-pointer when we uploaded parts of a\n     buffer, so the next read should read from where this pointer points to,\n     and the 'upload_present' contains the number of bytes available at this\n     position */\n  char *upload_fromhere;\n\n  /* Allocated protocol-specific data. Each protocol handler makes sure this\n     points to data it needs. */\n  union {\n    struct FILEPROTO *file;\n    struct FTP *ftp;\n    struct HTTP *http;\n    struct IMAP *imap;\n    struct ldapreqinfo *ldap;\n    struct MQTT *mqtt;\n    struct POP3 *pop3;\n    struct RTSP *rtsp;\n    struct smb_request *smb;\n    struct SMTP *smtp;\n    struct SSHPROTO *ssh;\n    struct TELNET *telnet;\n  } p;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\n  struct dohdata *doh; /* DoH specific data for this request */\n#endif\n#if defined(WIN32) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\n  struct curltime last_sndbuf_update;  /* last time readwrite_upload called\n                                          win_update_buffer_size */\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\n  unsigned char setcookies;\n#endif\n  unsigned char writer_stack_depth; /* Unencoding stack depth. */\n  BIT(header);        /* incoming data has HTTP header */\n  BIT(content_range); /* set TRUE if Content-Range: was found */\n  BIT(upload_done);   /* set to TRUE when doing chunked transfer-encoding\n                         upload and we're uploading the last chunk */\n  BIT(ignorebody);    /* we read a response-body but we ignore it! */\n  BIT(http_bodyless); /* HTTP response status code is between 100 and 199,\n                         204 or 304 */\n  BIT(chunk);         /* if set, this is a chunked transfer-encoding */\n  BIT(ignore_cl);     /* ignore content-length */\n  BIT(upload_chunky); /* set TRUE if we are doing chunked transfer-encoding\n                         on upload */\n  BIT(getheader);    /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\n  BIT(forbidchunk);  /* used only to explicitly forbid chunk-upload for\n                        specific upload buffers. See readmoredata() in http.c\n                        for details. */\n  BIT(no_body);      /* the response has no body */\n};\n\n/*\n * Specific protocol handler.\n */\n\nstruct Curl_handler {\n  const char *scheme;        /* URL scheme name. */\n\n  /* Complement to setup_connection_internals(). This is done before the\n     transfer \"owns\" the connection. */\n  CURLcode (*setup_connection)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  /* These two functions MUST be set to be protocol dependent */\n  CURLcode (*do_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*done)(struct Curl_easy *, CURLcode, bool);\n\n  /* If the curl_do() function is better made in two halves, this\n   * curl_do_more() function will be called afterwards, if set. For example\n   * for doing the FTP stuff after the PASV/PORT command.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*do_more)(struct Curl_easy *, int *);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * after the connect() and everything is done, as a step in the connection.\n   * The 'done' pointer points to a bool that should be set to TRUE if the\n   * function completes before return. If it doesn't complete, the caller\n   * should call the ->connecting() function until it is.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*connect_it)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* See above. */\n  CURLcode (*connecting)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n  CURLcode (*doing)(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the PROTOCONNECT phase, and it\n     should then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*proto_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                       struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DOING phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*doing_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                       struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_MORE phase, and it should\n     then return a proper fd set */\n  int (*domore_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                        struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* Called from the multi interface during the DO_DONE, PERFORM and\n     WAITPERFORM phases, and it should then return a proper fd set. Not setting\n     this will make libcurl use the generic default one. */\n  int (*perform_getsock)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);\n\n  /* This function *MAY* be set to a protocol-dependent function that is run\n   * by the curl_disconnect(), as a step in the disconnection.  If the handler\n   * is called because the connection has been considered dead,\n   * dead_connection is set to TRUE. The connection is (again) associated with\n   * the transfer here.\n   */\n  CURLcode (*disconnect)(struct Curl_easy *, struct connectdata *,\n                         bool dead_connection);\n\n  /* If used, this function gets called from transfer.c:readwrite_data() to\n     allow the protocol to do extra reads/writes */\n  CURLcode (*readwrite)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn,\n                        ssize_t *nread, bool *readmore);\n\n  /* This function can perform various checks on the connection. See\n     CONNCHECK_* for more information about the checks that can be performed,\n     and CONNRESULT_* for the results that can be returned. */\n  unsigned int (*connection_check)(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                   struct connectdata *conn,\n                                   unsigned int checks_to_perform);\n\n  /* attach() attaches this transfer to this connection */\n  void (*attach)(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);\n\n  int defport;            /* Default port. */\n  curl_prot_t protocol;  /* See CURLPROTO_* - this needs to be the single\n                            specific protocol bit */\n  curl_prot_t family;    /* single bit for protocol family; basically the\n                            non-TLS name of the protocol this is */\n  unsigned int flags;     /* Extra particular characteristics, see PROTOPT_* */\n\n};\n\n#define PROTOPT_NONE 0             /* nothing extra */\n#define PROTOPT_SSL (1<<0)         /* uses SSL */\n#define PROTOPT_DUAL (1<<1)        /* this protocol uses two connections */\n#define PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION (1<<2) /* need action before socket close */\n/* some protocols will have to call the underlying functions without regard to\n   what exact state the socket signals. IE even if the socket says \"readable\",\n   the send function might need to be called while uploading, or vice versa.\n*/\n#define PROTOPT_DIRLOCK (1<<3)\n#define PROTOPT_NONETWORK (1<<4)   /* protocol doesn't use the network! */\n#define PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD (1<<5)    /* needs a password, and if none is set it\n                                      gets a default */\n#define PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY (1<<6)   /* protocol can't handle\n                                        url query strings (?foo=bar) ! */\n#define PROTOPT_CREDSPERREQUEST (1<<7) /* requires login credentials per\n                                          request instead of per connection */\n#define PROTOPT_ALPN (1<<8) /* set ALPN for this */\n/* (1<<9) was PROTOPT_STREAM, now free */\n#define PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS (1<<10) /* allow options part in the userinfo field\n                                      of the URL */\n#define PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP (1<<11) /* allow this non-HTTP scheme over a\n                                         HTTP proxy as HTTP proxies may know\n                                         this protocol and act as a gateway */\n#define PROTOPT_WILDCARD (1<<12) /* protocol supports wildcard matching */\n#define PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL (1<<13) /* Allow \"control bytes\" (< 32 ascii) in\n                                       user name and password */\n#define PROTOPT_NOTCPPROXY (1<<14) /* this protocol can't proxy over TCP */\n\n#define CONNCHECK_NONE 0                 /* No checks */\n#define CONNCHECK_ISDEAD (1<<0)          /* Check if the connection is dead. */\n#define CONNCHECK_KEEPALIVE (1<<1)       /* Perform any keepalive function. */\n\n#define CONNRESULT_NONE 0                /* No extra information. */\n#define CONNRESULT_DEAD (1<<0)           /* The connection is dead. */\n\nstruct proxy_info {\n  struct hostname host;\n  int port;\n  unsigned char proxytype; /* curl_proxytype: what kind of proxy that is in\n                              use */\n  char *user;    /* proxy user name string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* proxy password string, allocated */\n};\n\nstruct ldapconninfo;\n\n#define TRNSPRT_TCP 3\n#define TRNSPRT_UDP 4\n#define TRNSPRT_QUIC 5\n#define TRNSPRT_UNIX 6\n\n/*\n * The connectdata struct contains all fields and variables that should be\n * unique for an entire connection.\n */\nstruct connectdata {\n  struct Curl_llist_element bundle_node; /* conncache */\n\n  /* chunk is for HTTP chunked encoding, but is in the general connectdata\n     struct only because we can do just about any protocol through an HTTP\n     proxy and an HTTP proxy may in fact respond using chunked encoding */\n  struct Curl_chunker chunk;\n\n  curl_closesocket_callback fclosesocket; /* function closing the socket(s) */\n  void *closesocket_client;\n\n  /* This is used by the connection cache logic. If this returns TRUE, this\n     handle is still used by one or more easy handles and can only used by any\n     other easy handle without careful consideration (== only for\n     multiplexing) and it cannot be used by another multi handle! */\n#define CONN_INUSE(c) ((c)->easyq.size)\n\n  /**** Fields set when inited and not modified again */\n  curl_off_t connection_id; /* Contains a unique number to make it easier to\n                               track the connections in the log output */\n\n  /* 'dns_entry' is the particular host we use. This points to an entry in the\n     DNS cache and it will not get pruned while locked. It gets unlocked in\n     multi_done(). This entry will be NULL if the connection is reused as then\n     there is no name resolve done. */\n  struct Curl_dns_entry *dns_entry;\n\n  /* 'remote_addr' is the particular IP we connected to. it is owned, set\n   * and NULLed by the connected socket filter (if there is one). */\n  const struct Curl_sockaddr_ex *remote_addr;\n\n  struct hostname host;\n  char *hostname_resolve; /* host name to resolve to address, allocated */\n  char *secondaryhostname; /* secondary socket host name (ftp) */\n  struct hostname conn_to_host; /* the host to connect to. valid only if\n                                   bits.conn_to_host is set */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct proxy_info socks_proxy;\n  struct proxy_info http_proxy;\n#endif\n  /* 'primary_ip' and 'primary_port' get filled with peer's numerical\n     ip address and port number whenever an outgoing connection is\n     *attempted* from the primary socket to a remote address. When more\n     than one address is tried for a connection these will hold data\n     for the last attempt. When the connection is actually established\n     these are updated with data which comes directly from the socket. */\n\n  char primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  char *user;    /* user name string, allocated */\n  char *passwd;  /* password string, allocated */\n  char *options; /* options string, allocated */\n  char *sasl_authzid;     /* authorization identity string, allocated */\n  char *oauth_bearer; /* OAUTH2 bearer, allocated */\n  struct curltime now;     /* \"current\" time */\n  struct curltime created; /* creation time */\n  struct curltime lastused; /* when returned to the connection cache */\n  curl_socket_t sock[2]; /* two sockets, the second is used for the data\n                            transfer when doing FTP */\n  Curl_recv *recv[2];\n  Curl_send *send[2];\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cfilter[2]; /* connection filters */\n\n  struct ssl_primary_config ssl_config;\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  struct ssl_primary_config proxy_ssl_config;\n#endif\n  struct ConnectBits bits;    /* various state-flags for this connection */\n\n  const struct Curl_handler *handler; /* Connection's protocol handler */\n  const struct Curl_handler *given;   /* The protocol first given */\n\n  /* Protocols can use a custom keepalive mechanism to keep connections alive.\n     This allows those protocols to track the last time the keepalive mechanism\n     was used on this connection. */\n  struct curltime keepalive;\n\n  /**** curl_get() phase fields */\n\n  curl_socket_t sockfd;   /* socket to read from or CURL_SOCKET_BAD */\n  curl_socket_t writesockfd; /* socket to write to, it may very\n                                well be the same we read from.\n                                CURL_SOCKET_BAD disables */\n\n#ifdef HAVE_GSSAPI\n  BIT(sec_complete); /* if Kerberos is enabled for this connection */\n  unsigned char command_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  unsigned char data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  unsigned char request_data_prot; /* enum protection_level */\n  size_t buffer_size;\n  struct krb5buffer in_buffer;\n  void *app_data;\n  const struct Curl_sec_client_mech *mech;\n  struct sockaddr_in local_addr;\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_KERBEROS5)    /* Consider moving some of the above GSS-API */\n  struct kerberos5data krb5;  /* variables into the structure definition, */\n#endif                        /* however, some of them are ftp specific. */\n\n  struct Curl_llist easyq;    /* List of easy handles using this connection */\n  curl_seek_callback seek_func; /* function that seeks the input */\n  void *seek_client;            /* pointer to pass to the seek() above */\n\n  /*************** Request - specific items ************/\n#if defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI) && defined(SECPKG_ATTR_ENDPOINT_BINDINGS)\n  CtxtHandle *sslContext;\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_GSASL\n  struct gsasldata gsasl;\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_NTLM)\n  curlntlm http_ntlm_state;\n  curlntlm proxy_ntlm_state;\n\n  struct ntlmdata ntlm;     /* NTLM differs from other authentication schemes\n                               because it authenticates connections, not\n                               single requests! */\n  struct ntlmdata proxyntlm; /* NTLM data for proxy */\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_SPNEGO\n  curlnegotiate http_negotiate_state;\n  curlnegotiate proxy_negotiate_state;\n\n  struct negotiatedata negotiate; /* state data for host Negotiate auth */\n  struct negotiatedata proxyneg; /* state data for proxy Negotiate auth */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_HTTP\n  /* for chunked-encoded trailer */\n  struct dynbuf trailer;\n#endif\n\n  union {\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\n    struct ftp_conn ftpc;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_SSH\n    struct ssh_conn sshc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_TFTP\n    struct tftp_state_data *tftpc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_IMAP\n    struct imap_conn imapc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_POP3\n    struct pop3_conn pop3c;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMTP\n    struct smtp_conn smtpc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_RTSP\n    struct rtsp_conn rtspc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_SMB\n    struct smb_conn smbc;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_LIBRTMP\n    void *rtmp;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_OPENLDAP\n    struct ldapconninfo *ldapc;\n#endif\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_MQTT\n    struct mqtt_conn mqtt;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_WEBSOCKETS\n    struct websocket *ws;\n#endif\n    unsigned int unused:1; /* avoids empty union */\n  } proto;\n\n  struct connectbundle *bundle; /* The bundle we are member of */\n#ifdef USE_UNIX_SOCKETS\n  char *unix_domain_socket;\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_HYPER\n  /* if set, an alternative data transfer function */\n  Curl_datastream datastream;\n#endif\n  /* When this connection is created, store the conditions for the local end\n     bind. This is stored before the actual bind and before any connection is\n     made and will serve the purpose of being used for comparison reasons so\n     that subsequent bound-requested connections aren't accidentally reusing\n     wrong connections. */\n  char *localdev;\n  unsigned short localportrange;\n  int waitfor;      /* current READ/WRITE bits to wait for */\n#if defined(HAVE_GSSAPI) || defined(USE_WINDOWS_SSPI)\n  int socks5_gssapi_enctype;\n#endif\n  /* The field below gets set in connect.c:connecthost() */\n  int port;        /* which port to use locally - to connect to */\n  int remote_port; /* the remote port, not the proxy port! */\n  int conn_to_port; /* the remote port to connect to. valid only if\n                       bits.conn_to_port is set */\n#ifdef ENABLE_IPV6\n  unsigned int scope_id;  /* Scope id for IPv6 */\n#endif\n  unsigned short localport;\n  unsigned short secondary_port; /* secondary socket remote port to connect to\n                                    (ftp) */\n  unsigned char cselect_bits; /* bitmask of socket events */\n  unsigned char alpn; /* APLN TLS negotiated protocol, a CURL_HTTP_VERSION*\n                         value */\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n  unsigned char proxy_alpn; /* APLN of proxy tunnel, CURL_HTTP_VERSION* */\n#endif\n  unsigned char transport; /* one of the TRNSPRT_* defines */\n  unsigned char ip_version; /* copied from the Curl_easy at creation time */\n  unsigned char httpversion; /* the HTTP version*10 reported by the server */\n  unsigned char connect_only;\n  unsigned char gssapi_delegation; /* inherited from set.gssapi_delegation */\n};\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_PROXY\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n          ((c)->bits.socksproxy ? (c)->socks_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n            (c)->bits.httpproxy ? (c)->http_proxy.host.dispname : \\\n              (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n                (c)->host.dispname)\n#else\n#define CURL_CONN_HOST_DISPNAME(c) \\\n          (c)->bits.conn_to_host ? (c)->conn_to_host.dispname : \\\n            (c)->host.dispname\n#endif\n\n/* The end of connectdata. */\n\n/*\n * Struct to keep statistical and informational data.\n * All variables in this struct must be initialized/reset in Curl_initinfo().\n */\nstruct PureInfo {\n  int httpcode;  /* Recent HTTP, FTP, RTSP or SMTP response code */\n  int httpproxycode; /* response code from proxy when received separate */\n  int httpversion; /* the http version number X.Y = X*10+Y */\n  time_t filetime; /* If requested, this is might get set. Set to -1 if the\n                      time was unretrievable. */\n  curl_off_t request_size; /* the amount of bytes sent in the request(s) */\n  unsigned long proxyauthavail; /* what proxy auth types were announced */\n  unsigned long httpauthavail;  /* what host auth types were announced */\n  long numconnects; /* how many new connection did libcurl created */\n  char *contenttype; /* the content type of the object */\n  char *wouldredirect; /* URL this would've been redirected to if asked to */\n  curl_off_t retry_after; /* info from Retry-After: header */\n  unsigned int header_size;  /* size of read header(s) in bytes */\n\n  /* PureInfo members 'conn_primary_ip', 'conn_primary_port', 'conn_local_ip'\n     and, 'conn_local_port' are copied over from the connectdata struct in\n     order to allow curl_easy_getinfo() to return this information even when\n     the session handle is no longer associated with a connection, and also\n     allow curl_easy_reset() to clear this information from the session handle\n     without disturbing information which is still alive, and that might be\n     reused, in the connection cache. */\n\n  char conn_primary_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  int conn_primary_port; /* this is the destination port to the connection,\n                            which might have been a proxy */\n  int conn_remote_port;  /* this is the \"remote port\", which is the port\n                            number of the used URL, independent of proxy or\n                            not */\n  char conn_local_ip[MAX_IPADR_LEN];\n  int conn_local_port;\n  const char *conn_scheme;\n  unsigned int conn_protocol;\n  struct curl_certinfo certs; /* info about the certs. Asked for with\n                                 CURLOPT_CERTINFO / CURLINFO_CERTINFO */\n  CURLproxycode pxcode;\n  BIT(timecond);  /* set to TRUE if the time condition didn't match, which\n                     thus made the document NOT get fetched */\n};\n\n\nstruct Progress {\n  time_t lastshow; /* time() of the last displayed progress meter or NULL to\n                      force redraw at next call */\n  curl_off_t size_dl; /* total expected size */\n  curl_off_t size_ul; /* total expected size */\n  curl_off_t downloaded; /* transferred so far */\n  curl_off_t uploaded; /* transferred so far */\n\n  curl_off_t current_speed; /* uses the currently fastest transfer */\n\n  int width; /* screen width at download start */\n  int flags; /* see progress.h */\n\n  timediff_t timespent;\n\n  curl_off_t dlspeed;\n  curl_off_t ulspeed;\n\n  timediff_t t_nslookup;\n  timediff_t t_connect;\n  timediff_t t_appconnect;\n  timediff_t t_pretransfer;\n  timediff_t t_starttransfer;\n  timediff_t t_redirect;\n\n  struct curltime start;\n  struct curltime t_startsingle;\n  struct curltime t_startop;\n  struct curltime t_acceptdata;\n\n\n  /* upload speed limit */\n  struct curltime ul_limit_start;\n  curl_off_t ul_limit_size;\n  /* download speed limit */\n  struct curltime dl_limit_start;\n  curl_off_t dl_limit_size;\n\n#define CURR_TIME (5 + 1) /* 6 entries for 5 seconds */\n\n  curl_off_t speeder[ CURR_TIME ];\n  struct curltime speeder_time[ CURR_TIME ];\n  int speeder_c;\n  BIT(callback);  /* set when progress callback is used */\n  BIT(is_t_startransfer_set);\n};\n\ntypedef enum {\n    RTSPREQ_NONE, /* first in list */\n    RTSPREQ_OPTIONS,\n    RTSPREQ_DESCRIBE,\n    RTSPREQ_ANNOUNCE,\n    RTSPREQ_SETUP,\n    RTSPREQ_PLAY,\n    RTSPREQ_PAUSE,\n    RTSPREQ_TEARDOWN,\n    RTSPREQ_GET_PARAMETER,\n    RTSPREQ_SET_PARAMETER,\n    RTSPREQ_RECORD,\n    RTSPREQ_RECEIVE,\n    RTSPREQ_LAST /* last in list */\n} Curl_RtspReq;\n\nstruct auth {\n  unsigned long want;  /* Bitmask set to the authentication methods wanted by\n                          app (with CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH or CURLOPT_PROXYAUTH). */\n  unsigned long picked;\n  unsigned long avail; /* Bitmask for what the server reports to support for\n                          this resource */\n  BIT(done);  /* TRUE when the auth phase is done and ready to do the\n                 actual request */\n  BIT(multipass); /* TRUE if this is not yet authenticated but within the\n                     auth multipass negotiation */\n  BIT(iestyle); /* TRUE if digest should be done IE-style or FALSE if it\n                   should be RFC compliant */\n};\n\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\nstruct Curl_data_prio_node {\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *next;\n  struct Curl_easy *data;\n};\n#endif\n\n/**\n * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others\n * on the same connection.\n * Supports both HTTP/2 priority and RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities.\n */\nstruct Curl_data_priority {\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */\n  struct Curl_easy *parent;\n  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;\n#endif\n  int weight;\n#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2\n  BIT(exclusive);\n#endif\n#if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */\n  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */\n  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */\n#endif\n};\n\n/*\n * This struct is for holding data that was attempted to get sent to the user's\n * callback but is held due to pausing. One instance per type (BOTH, HEADER,\n * BODY).\n */\nstruct tempbuf {\n  struct dynbuf b;\n  int type;   /* type of the 'tempwrite' buffer as a bitmask that is used with\n                 Curl_client_write() */\n  BIT(paused_body); /* if PAUSE happened before/during BODY write */\n};\n\n/* Timers */\ntypedef enum {\n  EXPIRE_100_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_ASYNC_NAME,\n  EXPIRE_CONNECTTIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME, /* family1 */\n  EXPIRE_DNS_PER_NAME2, /* family2 */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS_DNS, /* See asyn-ares.c */\n  EXPIRE_HAPPY_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_MULTI_PENDING,\n  EXPIRE_RUN_NOW,\n  EXPIRE_SPEEDCHECK,\n  EXPIRE_TIMEOUT,\n  EXPIRE_TOOFAST,\n  EXPIRE_QUIC,\n  EXPIRE_FTP_ACCEPT,\n  EXPIRE_ALPN_EYEBALLS,\n  EXPIRE_LAST /* not an actual timer, used as a marker only */\n} expire_id;\n\n\ntypedef enum {\n  TRAILERS_NONE,\n  TRAILERS_INITIALIZED,\n  TRAILERS_SENDING,\n  TRAILERS_DONE\n} trailers_state;\n\n\n/*\n * One instance for each timeout an easy handle can set.\n */\nstruct time_node {\n  struct Curl_llist_element list;\n  struct curltime time;\n  expire_id eid;\n};\n\n/* individual pieces of the URL */\nstruct urlpieces {\n  char *scheme;\n  char *hostname;\n  char *port;\n  char *user;\n  char *password;\n  char *options;\n  char *path;\n  char *query;\n};\n\nstruct UrlState {\n  /* Points to the connection cache */\n  struct conncache *conn_cache;\n  /* buffers to store authentication data in, as parsed from input options */\n  struct curltime keeps_speed; /* for the progress meter really */\n\n  curl_off_t lastconnect_id; /* The last connection, -1 if undefined */\n  curl_off_t recent_conn_id; /* The most recent connection used, might no\n                              * longer exist */\n  struct dynbuf headerb; /* buffer to store headers in */\n\n  char *buffer; /* download buffer */\n  char *ulbuf; /* allocated upload buffer or NULL */\n  curl_off_t current_speed;  /* the ProgressShow() function sets this,\n                                bytes / second */\n\n  /* host name, port number and protocol of the first (not followed) request.\n     if set, this should be the host name that we will sent authorization to,\n     no else. Used to make Location: following not keep sending user+password.\n     This is strdup()ed data. */\n  char *first_host;\n  int first_remote_port;\n  curl_prot_t first_remote_protocol;\n\n  int retrycount; /* number of retries on a new connection */\n  struct Curl_ssl_session *session; /* array of 'max_ssl_sessions' size */\n  long sessionage;                  /* number of the most recent session */\n  struct tempbuf tempwrite[3];
