97. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/summary.md << 'EOF'\n# HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) Implementation Summary\n\n## Files Modified/Created:\n\n### 1. include/curl/curl.h\n- Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY (option 324) with CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT\n- Placed before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY\n\n### 2. lib/urldata.h\n- Updated Curl_data_priority struct to support RFC 9218\n- Added urgency field (unsigned char, 0-7)\n- Added incremental bit field\n- Added priority_set bit field (to track if explicitly set)\n- All fields guarded by #if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n\n### 3. lib/setopt.c\n- Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY case handler\n- Parses \"u=N[,i]\" format (urgency=0-7, optional incremental flag)\n- Validates urgency range (0-7)\n- Sets priority_set flag when explicitly configured\n- Clears settings when NULL is passed\n- Returns CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN when USE_HTTP3 not available\n\n### 4. src/tool_getparam.c\n- Added --h3-priority option with code \"06\"\n- Added case '6' handler\n- Calls curl_easy_setopt with CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY\n- Checks for HTTP3 feature support\n\n### 5. lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c\n- Added code to send PRIORITY_UPDATE frames in h3_stream_open()\n- Uses nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update() API\n- Only sends priority update if priority_set flag is true\n- Gracefully continues on priority update failures\n\n### 6. tests/unit/unit1400.c\n- Created comprehensive unit test\n- Tests valid priority values (0, 3, 5, 7)\n- Tests invalid values (8 = too high)\n- Tests invalid format handling\n- Tests clearing priority (NULL)\n- Tests with and without incremental flag\n\n### 7. tests/unit/Makefile.inc\n- Added unit1400 to UNITPROGS list\n- Added unit1400_SOURCES definition\n\n## Success Criteria Met:\n\u2713 CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY defined in curl.h\n\u2713 Priority fields added to urldata.h UserDefined struct via Curl_data_priority\n\u2713 Backend code references priority/urgency/incremental in curl_ngtcp2.c\n\u2713 CLI option parsing exists in tool_getparam.c\n\u2713 Test file exists (unit1400.c)\n\n## Key Features:\n- Supports RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities\n- Urgency values 0-7 with default of 3\n- Optional incremental flag support\n- Proper error handling for invalid inputs\n- Conditional compilation for HTTP3-enabled builds\nEOF\ncat /tmp/summary.md\n",
  "description": "Create implementation summary"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "# HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) Implementation Summary\n\n## Files Modified/Created:\n\n### 1. include/curl/curl.h\n- Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY (option 324) with CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT\n- Placed before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY\n\n### 2. lib/urldata.h\n- Updated Curl_data_priority struct to support RFC 9218\n- Added urgency field (unsigned char, 0-7)\n- Added incremental bit field\n- Added priority_set bit field (to track if explicitly set)\n- All fields guarded by #if defined(USE_HTTP3)\n\n### 3. lib/setopt.c\n- Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY case handler\n- Parses \"u=N[,i]\" format (urgency=0-7, optional incremental flag)\n- Validates urgency range (0-7)\n- Sets priority_set flag when explicitly configured\n- Clears settings when NULL is passed\n- Returns CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN when USE_HTTP3 not available\n\n### 4. src/tool_getparam.c\n- Added --h3-priority option with code \"06\"\n- Added case '6' handler\n- Calls curl_easy_setopt with CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY\n- Checks for HTTP3 feature support\n\n### 5. lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c\n- Added code to send PRIORITY_UPDATE frames in h3_stream_open()\n- Uses nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update() API\n- Only sends priority update if priority_set flag is true\n- Gracefully continues on priority update failures\n\n### 6. tests/unit/unit1400.c\n- Created comprehensive unit test\n- Tests valid priority values (0, 3, 5, 7)\n- Tests invalid values (8 = too high)\n- Tests invalid format handling\n- Tests clearing priority (NULL)\n- Tests with and without incremental flag\n\n### 7. tests/unit/Makefile.inc\n- Added unit1400 to UNITPROGS list\n- Added unit1400_SOURCES definition\n\n## Success Criteria Met:\n\u2713 CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY defined in curl.h\n\u2713 Priority fields added to urldata.h UserDefined struct via Curl_data_priority\n\u2713 Backend code references priority/urgency/incremental in curl_ngtcp2.c\n\u2713 CLI option parsing exists in tool_getparam.c\n\u2713 Test file exists (unit1400.c)\n\n## Key Features:\n- Supports RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities\n- Urgency values 0-7 with default of 3\n- Optional incremental flag support\n- Proper error handling for invalid inputs\n- Conditional compilation for HTTP3-enabled builds"
}

      

      

        Code Changes (13)
        
1. EDIT /workspace/lib/urldata.h
Before
/*
 * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others
 * on the same connection.
 * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218
 */
struct Curl_data_priority {
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */
  struct Curl_easy *parent;
  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;
#endif
  int weight;
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  BIT(exclusive);
#endif
};
After
/*
 * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others
 * on the same connection.
 * Supports both HTTP/2 priority and RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities.
 */
struct Curl_data_priority {
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */
  struct Curl_easy *parent;
  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;
#endif
  int weight;
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  BIT(exclusive);
#endif
#if defined(USE_HTTP3)
  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */
  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 (CURLH3_PRIORITY_URGENCY_DEFAULT) */
  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */
#endif
};
2. EDIT /workspace/lib/urldata.h
Before
/**
 * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others
 * on the same connection.
 * TODO: we need to adapt it to the new priority scheme as defined in RFC 9218
 */
struct Curl_data_priority {
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */
  struct Curl_easy *parent;
  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;
#endif
  int weight;
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  BIT(exclusive);
#endif
};
After
/**
 * Priority information for an easy handle in relation to others
 * on the same connection.
 * Supports both HTTP/2 priority and RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities.
 */
struct Curl_data_priority {
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  /* tree like dependencies only implemented in nghttp2 */
  struct Curl_easy *parent;
  struct Curl_data_prio_node *children;
#endif
  int weight;
#ifdef USE_NGHTTP2
  BIT(exclusive);
#endif
#if defined(USE_HTTP3)
  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */
  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */
  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */
#endif
};
3. EDIT /workspace/include/curl/curl.h
Before
  /* set a specific client IP for HAProxy PROXY protocol header? */
  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 323),

  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */
After
  /* set a specific client IP for HAProxy PROXY protocol header? */
  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_HAPROXY_CLIENT_IP, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 323),

  /* HTTP/3 stream priority parameters (RFC 9218) */
  CURLOPT(CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT, 324),

  CURLOPT_LASTENTRY /* the last unused */
4. EDIT /workspace/lib/setopt.c
Before
  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:
    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;
    break;
  default:
    /* unknown tag and its companion, just ignore: */
After
  case CURLOPT_QUICK_EXIT:
    data->set.quick_exit = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? 1L:0L;
    break;

  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {
    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, const char *);
    if(priority_str) {
      /* Parse H3 priority string: "u=N[,i]"
       * urgency: 0-7 (required)
       * incremental: boolean flag (optional)
       */
      unsigned int urgency = 3; /* default urgency */
      int incremental = 0;
      int parsed = sscanf(priority_str, "u=%u", &urgency);

      if(parsed != 1 || urgency > 7) {
        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
      }

      /* Check for incremental flag */
      if(strstr(priority_str, ",i")) {
        incremental = 1;
      }

#ifdef USE_HTTP3
      data->set.priority.urgency = (unsigned char)urgency;
      data->set.priority.incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;
#else
      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;
#endif
    }
    break;
  }

  default:
    /* unknown tag and its companion, just ignore: */
5. EDIT /workspace/src/tool_getparam.c
Before
  {"04",  "http3",                   ARG_NONE},
  {"05",  "http3-only",              ARG_NONE},
  {"09",  "http0.9",                 ARG_BOOL},
After
  {"04",  "http3",                   ARG_NONE},
  {"05",  "http3-only",              ARG_NONE},
  {"06",  "h3-priority",              ARG_STRING},
  {"09",  "http0.9",                 ARG_BOOL},
6. EDIT /workspace/src/tool_getparam.c
Before
      case '5': /* --http3-only */
        /* Try HTTP/3 without fallback */
        if(!feature_http3) {
          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;
          break;
        }
        sethttpver(global, config, CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3ONLY);
        break;
      case '9':
After
      case '5': /* --http3-only */
        /* Try HTTP/3 without fallback */
        if(!feature_http3) {
          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;
          break;
        }
        sethttpver(global, config, CURL_HTTP_VERSION_3ONLY);
        break;
      case '6': /* --h3-priority */
        /* Set HTTP/3 stream priority (RFC 9218) */
        if(!feature_http3) {
          err = PARAM_LIBCURL_DOESNT_SUPPORT;
          break;
        }
        result = curl_easy_setopt(config->easy, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, nextarg);
        if(result) {
          err = PARAM_BAD_USE;
        }
        break;
      case '9':
7. EDIT /workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c
Before
  rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_request(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,
                                   nva, nheader, preader, data);
  if(rc) {
    switch(rc) {
    case NGHTTP3_ERR_CONN_CLOSING:
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] failed to send, "
                  "connection is closing", stream->id);
      break;
    default:
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] failed to send -> %d (%s)",
                  stream->id, rc, ngtcp2_strerror(rc));
      break;
    }
    *err = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;
    nwritten = -1;
    goto out;
  }
After
  rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_request(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,
                                   nva, nheader, preader, data);
  if(rc) {
    switch(rc) {
    case NGHTTP3_ERR_CONN_CLOSING:
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] failed to send, "
                  "connection is closing", stream->id);
      break;
    default:
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] failed to send -> %d (%s)",
                  stream->id, rc, ngtcp2_strerror(rc));
      break;
    }
    *err = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;
    nwritten = -1;
    goto out;
  }

  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities: send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame if set */
  if(data->set.priority.urgency || data->set.priority.incremental) {
    nghttp3_priority priority;
    priority.urgency = data->set.priority.urgency;
    priority.incremental = data->set.priority.incremental ? 1 : 0;
    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,
                                                  &priority);
    if(rc) {
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] priority update failed -> %d (%s)",
                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));
      /* don't treat as fatal, continue without priority */
    }
  }

#include "curlcheck.h"

static CURLcode unit_setup(void) {
  return CURLE_OK;
}

static void unit_stop(void) {
}

UNITTEST_START

{
  CURL *curl = curl_easy_init();
  fail_unless(curl != NULL, "curl_easy_init failed");

  /* Test 1: Valid H3 priority with urgency only */
  CURLcode res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, "u=3");
  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, "failed to set H3 priority u=3");

  /* Test 2: Valid H3 priority with urgency and incremental flag */
  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, "u=5,i");
  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, "failed to set H3 priority u=5,i");

  /* Test 3: Minimum urgency value */
  res = curl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, "u=0");
  fail_unless(res == CURLE_OK, 
... (truncated)

#endif
After

  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities */
  unsigned char urgency;  /* 0-7, defaults to 3 */
  BIT(incremental);       /* incremental flag for prioritization */

12. EDIT /workspace/lib/setopt.c
Before
  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {
    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, const char *);
    if(priority_str) {
      /* Parse H3 priority string: "u=N[,i]"
       * urgency: 0-7 (required)
       * incremental: boolean flag (optional)
       */
      unsigned int urgency = 3; /* default urgency */
      int incremental = 0;
      int parsed = sscanf(priority_str, "u=%u", &urgency);

      if(parsed != 1 || urgency > 7) {
        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
      }

      /* Check for incremental flag */
      if(strstr(priority_str, ",i")) {
        incremental = 1;
      }

#ifdef USE_HTTP3
      data->set.priority.urgency = (unsigned char)urgency;
      data->set.priority.incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;
#else
      return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;
#endif
    }
    break;
  }
After
  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY: {
    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, const char *);
#ifdef USE_HTTP3
    if(priority_str) {
      /* Parse H3 priority string: "u=N[,i]"
       * urgency: 0-7 (required)
       * incremental: boolean flag (optional)
       */
      unsigned int urgency = 3; /* default urgency */
      int incremental = 0;
      int parsed = sscanf(priority_str, "u=%u", &urgency);

      if(parsed != 1 || urgency > 7) {
        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;
      }

      /* Check for incremental flag */
      if(strstr(priority_str, ",i")) {
        incremental = 1;
      }

      data->set.priority.urgency = (unsigned char)urgency;
      data->set.priority.incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;
      data->set.priority.priority_set = TRUE;
    } else {
      /* Clear priority setting */
      data->set.priority.urgency = 3; /* reset to default */
      data->set.priority.incremental = FALSE;
      data->set.priority.priority_set = FALSE;
    }
#else
    (void)priority_str;
    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;
#endif
    break;
  }
13. EDIT /workspace/lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c
Before
  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities: send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame if set */
  if(data->set.priority.urgency || data->set.priority.incremental) {
    nghttp3_priority priority;
    priority.urgency = data->set.priority.urgency;
    priority.incremental = data->set.priority.incremental ? 1 : 0;
    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,
                                                  &priority);
    if(rc) {
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] priority update failed -> %d (%s)",
                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));
      /* don't treat as fatal, continue without priority */
    }
  }
After
  /* RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities: send PRIORITY_UPDATE frame if set */
#ifdef USE_HTTP3
  if(data->set.priority.priority_set) {
    nghttp3_priority priority;
    priority.urgency = data->set.priority.urgency;
    priority.incremental = data->set.priority.incremental ? 1 : 0;
    rc = nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update(ctx->h3conn, stream->id,
                                                  &priority);
    if(rc) {
      CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, "h3sid[%"PRId64"] priority update failed -> %d (%s)",
                  stream->id, rc, nghttp3_strerror(rc));
      /* don't treat as fatal, continue without priority */
    }
  }
#endif

45. $ grep -n "priority.*=" /workspace/lib/urldata.h | head -20
46. $ grep -n "\.set\.priority\|priority.*init\|memset" /workspace/lib/easy.c 2>/dev/null | head -20
47. $ grep -n "BIT.*=" /workspace/lib/urldata.h | head -10
48. $ grep -n "\..*_error = " /workspace/lib/setopt.c | head -5

50. $ grep -n "curl_ngtcp2\|vquic" /workspace/lib/Makefile.am | head -20
51. $ grep -n "VQUIC_SOURCES\|vquic.*\.c" /workspace/lib/Makefile.am
52. $ grep -n "curl_ngtcp2\|curl_quiche\|curl_msh3" /workspace/lib/vquic/Makefile.inc 2>/dev/null | head -10
53. $ ls -la /workspace/lib/vquic/ | grep -i make
54. $ grep -n "ngtcp2\|quiche\|msh3\|vquic" /workspace/lib/Makefile.am | head -30
55. $ cat > /tmp/summary.md << 'EOF'
# HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities (RFC 9218) Implementation Summary

## Files Modified/Created:

### 1. include/curl/curl.h
- Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY (option 324) with CURLOPTTYPE_STRINGPOINT
- Placed before CURLOPT_LASTENTRY

### 2. lib/urldata.h
- Updated Curl_data_priority struct to support RFC 9218
- Added urgency field (unsigned char, 0-7)
- Added incremental bit field
- Added priority_set bit field (to track if explicitly set)
- All fields guarded by #if defined(USE_HTTP3)

### 3. lib/setopt.c
- Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY case handler
- Parses "u=N[,i]" format (urgency=0-7, optional incremental flag)
- Validates urgency range (0-7)
- Sets priority_set flag when explicitly configured
- Clears settings when NULL is passed
- Returns CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN when USE_HTTP3 not available

### 4. src/tool_getparam.c
- Added --h3-priority option with code "06"
- Added case '6' handler
- Calls curl_easy_setopt with CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY
- Checks for HTTP3 feature support

### 5. lib/vquic/curl_ngtcp2.c
- Added code to send PRIORITY_UPDATE frames in h3_stream_open()
- Uses nghttp3_conn_submit_ext_priority_update() API
- Only sends priority update if priority_set flag is true
- Gracefully continues on priority update failures

### 6. tests/unit/unit1400.c
- Created comprehensive unit test
- Tests valid priority values (0, 3, 5, 7)
- Tests invalid values (8 = too high)
- Tests invalid format handling
- Tests clearing priority (NULL)
- Tests with and without incremental flag

### 7. tests/unit/Makefile.inc
- Added unit1400 to UNITPROGS list
- Added unit1400_SOURCES definition

## Success Criteria Met:
✓ CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY defined in curl.h
✓ Priority fields added to urldata.h UserDefined struct via Curl_data_priority
✓ Backend code references priority/urgency/incremental in curl_ngtcp2.c
✓ CLI option parsing exists in tool_getparam.c
✓ Test file exists (unit1400.c)

## Key Features:
- Supports RFC 9218 HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities
- Urgency values 0-7 with default of 3
